questions.question: Tama is on an interview panel and one of the applicants is his cousin. What type of conflict of interest does this represent?
questions.questionHtml: Tama is on an interview panel and one of the applicants is his cousin. What type of conflict of interest does this represent?

answers.text: No conflict — Tama can remain objective
answers.textHtml: No conflict — Tama can remain objective

answers.text: A perceived conflict of interest
answers.textHtml: A perceived conflict of interest

answers.text: A financial conflict of interest
answers.textHtml: A financial conflict of interest

answers.text: Only a conflict if Tama's cousin gets the job
answers.textHtml: Only a conflict if Tama's cousin gets the job

questions.feedback: This is a perceived conflict of interest. Even if Tama believes he can be fair, a reasonable person would question whether the family relationship influenced the outcome. Tama should disclose the relationship and step back from assessing his cousin's application.
questions.feedbackHtml: This is a perceived conflict of interest. Even if Tama believes he can be fair, a reasonable person would question whether the family relationship influenced the outcome. Tama should disclose the relationship and step back from assessing his cousin's application.

questions.question: What is the first thing you should do when you identify a conflict of interest?
questions.questionHtml: What is the first thing you should do when you identify a conflict of interest?

answers.text: Manage it yourself without telling anyone to avoid embarrassment
answers.textHtml: Manage it yourself without telling anyone to avoid embarrassment

answers.text: Wait until the decision is made, then disclose
answers.textHtml: Wait until the decision is made, then disclose

answers.text: Disclose it to your manager as soon as possible
answers.textHtml: Disclose it to your manager as soon as possible

answers.text: Resign from your position
answers.textHtml: Resign from your position

questions.feedback: You should disclose the conflict to your manager as soon as you become aware of it. Early disclosure allows time to put appropriate management in place before any decision is made. Managing a conflict on your own, or waiting until after a decision, undermines trust and integrity.
questions.feedbackHtml: You should disclose the conflict to your manager as soon as you become aware of it. Early disclosure allows time to put appropriate management in place before any decision is made. Managing a conflict on your own, or waiting until after a decision, undermines trust and integrity.

questions.question: Select ALL that apply: Which of the following are examples of personal interests that can create a conflict of interest?
questions.questionHtml: Select ALL that apply: Which of the following are examples of personal interests that can create a conflict of interest?

answers.text: Owning shares in a company your agency regulates
answers.textHtml: Owning shares in a company your agency regulates

answers.text: A family member applying for a job in your team
answers.textHtml: A family member applying for a job in your team

answers.text: Having lunch with a colleague
answers.textHtml: Having lunch with a colleague

answers.text: Running a private business in the same area you work in professionally
answers.textHtml: Running a private business in the same area you work in professionally

answers.text: A close friend's employer bidding on a government contract you manage
answers.textHtml: A close friend's employer bidding on a government contract you manage

questions.feedback: Financial interests (like shares), family relationships in recruitment, secondary employment in your professional area, and relationships with parties to procurement decisions can all create conflicts. Simply having lunch with a colleague does not create a conflict of interest.
questions.feedbackHtml: Financial interests (like shares), family relationships in recruitment, secondary employment in your professional area, and relationships with parties to procurement decisions can all create conflicts. Simply having lunch with a colleague does not create a conflict of interest.

questions.question: Sandra is a grants manager. Her sister's organisation has applied for a grant from her agency. What should Sandra do?
questions.questionHtml: Sandra is a grants manager. Her sister's organisation has applied for a grant from her agency. What should Sandra do?

answers.text: Assess the application fairly — family relationships don't affect professional judgment
answers.textHtml: Assess the application fairly — family relationships don't affect professional judgment

answers.text: Decline the application to avoid any appearance of favouritism
answers.textHtml: Decline the application to avoid any appearance of favouritism

answers.text: Disclose the conflict and transfer responsibility for that application to a colleague
answers.textHtml: Disclose the conflict and transfer responsibility for that application to a colleague

answers.text: Ask her sister to withdraw the application
answers.textHtml: Ask her sister to withdraw the application

questions.feedback: Sandra should disclose the conflict to her manager and transfer the application to a colleague who has no personal connection to the applicant. She should not assess the application herself — even if she believes she can be fair — and she should not use her position to influence the outcome in either direction.
questions.feedbackHtml: Sandra should disclose the conflict to her manager and transfer the application to a colleague who has no personal connection to the applicant. She should not assess the application herself — even if she believes she can be fair — and she should not use her position to influence the outcome in either direction.

questions.question: Match each conflict of interest management option to its description:
questions.questionHtml: Match each conflict of interest management option to its description:

answers.text: Recuse yourself
answers.textHtml: Recuse yourself
answers.matchText: Step back from a specific decision or process entirely
answers.matchTextHtml: Step back from a specific decision or process entirely

answers.text: Divest the interest
answers.textHtml: Divest the interest
answers.matchText: Sell shares or other financial holdings to remove the conflict
answers.matchTextHtml: Sell shares or other financial holdings to remove the conflict

answers.text: Acknowledge and monitor
answers.textHtml: Acknowledge and monitor
answers.matchText: Document the conflict and continue with heightened transparency
answers.matchTextHtml: Document the conflict and continue with heightened transparency

answers.text: Transfer responsibility
answers.textHtml: Transfer responsibility
answers.matchText: Permanently assign that area of work to a colleague
answers.matchTextHtml: Permanently assign that area of work to a colleague

questions.feedback: There are multiple ways to manage a conflict of interest. The right approach depends on the severity of the conflict. Recusing yourself means stepping back from a specific decision; divesting means removing a financial interest; acknowledging and monitoring is appropriate for lower-risk conflicts; and transferring responsibility is used when the conflict is ongoing.
questions.feedbackHtml: There are multiple ways to manage a conflict of interest. The right approach depends on the severity of the conflict. Recusing yourself means stepping back from a specific decision; divesting means removing a financial interest; acknowledging and monitoring is appropriate for lower-risk conflicts; and transferring responsibility is used when the conflict is ongoing.
